title: Rewrite contracts and training as native SIGSEGV institutions
description: Replace fantasy-era quest hall and level-master framing with original SIGSEGV fiction built around contracts, operators, specialization, and hostile runtime survival. Focus on player-facing copy and interaction framing only; keep internal IDs, persistence, and world-sim routing stable.

title: Pin supported .NET SDK for reproducible builds
description: Add an explicit SDK pin or documented bootstrap path for this repository. The project targets net8.0, but validation on a machine with only the .NET 10 SDK available stalled during restore while attempting to resolve 8.0 targeting packs. This was discovered while validating the SIGSEGV rebrand foundation.

title: Re-theme web and multiplayer surfaces for SIGSEGV
description: Update website copy, browser terminal entry points, package metadata, and online/BBS-facing text so the fork stops advertising itself as Usurper Reborn while preserving safe deployment defaults. This work was identified while establishing the initial rebrand foundation.

title: Migrate legacy gameplay terminology to SIGSEGV vocabulary
description: Replace player-facing fantasy terminology, locations, and progression language in localization, menus, and core narrative flows with the SIGSEGV command-console, lattice, payload, entropy, and subnet vocabulary. This work was identified while establishing the initial rebrand foundation.

title: Establish SIGSEGV rebrand foundation
description: Create the initial SIGSEGV project identity for the fork: replace top-level branding, add canonical lore and threat-model docs, and update console-facing title text without claiming full gameplay conversion.

title: Track remaining English gameplay fiction migration
description: Core loop terminology has been migrated for combat, the command console hub, shared location display names, and world-sim activity text. Remaining English player-facing copy still mixes fantasy-era terms (companions, story arcs, achievements, NPC story text, and secondary prompts). Continue the SIGSEGV fiction migration in presentation-only layers without renaming internal location tokens or save-facing identifiers.

title: Plan SIGSEGV hostname and endpoint migration
description: Public branding now says SIGSEGV, but live endpoints and deployment defaults still point at usurper-reborn.net and play.usurper-reborn.net. Do not change these blindly: they cross trust boundaries and would break clients unless DNS, TLS, nginx, SSH entrypoints, release metadata, and web proxy allowlists are cut over together. Track the coordinated migration here.
